Product Description**\\nBright Data provides a comprehensive web data platform. Its **Web MCP (Model Context Protocol)** server is an all-in-one solution designed to connect AI agents and LLMs to the internet. It enables real-time web search, data extraction (markdown/HTML), navigation, and interaction while bypassing geo-restrictions, CAPTCHAs, and anti-bot protections.\\n\\n**2. API & Developer Documentation**\\n- **Documentation Hub:** [https://docs.brightdata.com/](https://docs.brightdata.com/)\\n- **MCP Documentation:** [https://docs.brightdata.com/ai/mcp-server/overview](https://docs.brightdata.com/ai/mcp-server/overview)\\n- **API Reference:** [https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/introduction](https://docs.brightdata.com/api-reference/introduction)\\n\\n**3. Authentication Methods**\\n- **API Token:** Primary method for MCP and administrative APIs. Authenticate via `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>` header or `API_TOKEN` environment variable for the MCP server.\\n- **Proxy Credentials:** Native access using `brd-customer-<ID>-zone-<ZONE>:<PASSWORD>` for proxy-based scraping products.\\n\\n**4. Pricing ...[truncated]

raw_result.description: Web data and scraping platform offering APIs, proxy infrastructure, and a Web MCP server for AI agents to search, extract, and interact with websites.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show bearer token auth for the MCP/API flow (`Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>` / `API_TOKEN`) and username:password-style proxy credentials (`brd-customer-<ID>-zone-<ZONE>:<PASSWORD>`) for proxy products.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official MCP materials describe a Rapid free tier with 5,000 free monthly requests/credits and self-serve setup, indicating developers can start without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer docs include an API reference plus multiple scraping/web-data services and MCP access; the gathered evidence supports a broad HTTP API surface for web data extraction and related tooling.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Bright Data has an official Web MCP server, with first-party docs and an official GitHub repository (`brightdata/brightdata-mcp`).

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered first-party evidence indicates the local MCP server supports a configurable `RATE_LIMIT`, with a documented default noted as `100/1h`; product/API limits may vary by service and account tier.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party MCP docs, a hosted MCP endpoint, free self-serve usage, and an official GitHub repo are strong signals of current agent-toolkit readiness.
